item.goetyawaken.bakasmusic_disc.desc: チルノのパーフェクトさんすう教室
item.goetyawaken.death_ray_focus.info: Launches a Death Ray in the direction the caster is looking.
item.goetyawaken.shulker_missile_focus.i: Launches tracking shulker missiles at targets.
item.goetyawaken.creeper_focus.info: Summons creeper servant for the caster.
item.goetyawaken.stare_focus.info: Summons enderman servant for the caster.
item.goetyawaken.torment_focus.info: Summons tormentor servant for the caster.
item.goetyawaken.infestation_focus.info: Launches silverfish eggs that hatch into silverfish servants for the caster.
item.goetyawaken.killer_focus.info: Summons killer rabbit servants for the caster.
item.goetyawaken.wololo_focus.info: Turns sheep wool red (blue when sneaking).
item.goetyawaken.blood_rain_focus.info: Summon Hell Clouds to bring forth a blood rain, inflicting scalding damage upon the creatures below.
item.goetyawaken.fairy_focus.info: Summons Maid Fairy Servant for the caster.
Launches a mushroom missile in the direction the caster is looking
item.goetyawaken.desert_plagues_focus.in: Unleash divine calamities, upon them with locusts, hail, and lightning.
item.goetyawaken.heaven_rift_focus.info: Calls down Pure Light from the sky to strike nearby enemies.
item.goetyawaken.champion_focus.info: Summons Rearguard for the caster. Only the lich player can cast this spell.
item.goetyawaken.marble_focus.info: Places a block of Heavy Silt Marble at the crosshair position.
item.goetyawaken.access_focus.info: Right-click an Ender Access Lectern to bind. Hold and right-click or cast via wand to open the bound storage network remotely. Sneak + right-click to unbind.
item.goetyawaken.access_focus.tooltip.un: Not bound to any Ender Access Lectern
item.goetyawaken.access_focus.bound: Bound to Ender Access Lectern at [%d, %d, %d]
item.goetyawaken.access_focus.not_bound: Access Focus is not bound to any Ender Access Lectern
item.goetyawaken.access_focus.lectern_mi: The bound Ender Access Lectern does not exist.
item.goetyawaken.mushroom_hat.tooltip: Grants immunity to poison and acid venom, provides resistance to negative effects, and actively attacks enemies
tooltip.goetyawaken.champion_set.4piece: 4 Pieces: Gain §6Shielding§r effect, immune to fall damage
item.goetyawaken.death_cap_mushroom.tool: Makes you berserk after consumption, can be used up to 10 times
item.goetyawaken.grave_bane.tooltip: The Smite enchantment will have twice the effectiveness
item.goetyawaken.gilded_ingot.descriptio: Can be combined with enchanted items on an anvil to increase all enchantment levels by 1

death.attack.goetyawaken.mushroom_missil: %1$s was blown to fragments by %2$s's mushroom missile!
death.attack.goetyawaken.explosive_arrow: %1$s faded away due to %2$s's explosive arrow
death.attack.goetyawaken.death_fire.burn: %1$s was burned to ashes by death fire
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.1: Disturbing someone's slumber isn't exactly polite, even for a soulless dark mage like yourself. Lucky for you, I'm in a good mood—otherwise, that Killing Spell would've been the first thing you heard this morning.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.2: You know what they say: you can't keep a good skeleton down. Or a mediocre one. Or any skeleton, really.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.3: Death is just a minor inconvenience when you have backup bodies. Like changing clothes, but bonier.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.4: Back from the death, slightly crispy, fully motivated. Who's next on our list of people who need to learn some manners?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.5: You know, most powerful beings get to stay dead. I'm starting to feel personally attacked.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.6: You know what they say: what doesn't kill you... just makes you find a new body and carry on.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.7: Ancient beasts and mighty wizards get their beauty sleep. Meanwhile, I'm here, running an undead kingdom on spite. Maybe... maybe this is my curse?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.8: Being summoned is always awkward. Do I bow? Do I curse your bloodline? I never quite remember the protocol.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.9: Ah, back among the living. Or... what passes for it. Did someone call for a necromancy expert? Or just the neighborhood's most stylish skeleton?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.10: Being summoned feels like waking up after a thousand-year nap with a crick in every single bone. Which, admittedly, is a lot of bones.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.11: I see you've mastered the art of necromancy. Good for you. Now, point me at something that needs a permanent attitude adjustment.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.12: Ah, back from the void. Did someone miss me? ...No? Rude.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.13: The Nameless One returns. Still nameless, but slightly more fabulous.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.14: Back from the dead. Again. At this point, it's just a commute.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.15: Being summoned feels like waking up from a nap you never asked for.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.16: You know, death is restful. Then someone always wants to start a kingdom again. Fine, but I'm keeping the throne.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.17: Another day, another chance to remind mortals why they fear the dark. And why they should really invest in better torches.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.18: Ah, the sweet scent of resurrection. Smells like... dust, old bones, and someone's impending regret.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.19: Did I miss anything interesting? Polarice3 updates goety? New dimensions? ...Another Herobrine sighting? No? Just another Tuesday in Minecraft, then.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.20: Death is not the end. It's just a really, really long loading screen. And I've finally spawned back in.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.21: Ah... another nap interrupted. You better have souls Or enemies. Preferably both.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.22: Behold! The Nameless One arises! ...Again. This is getting to be a habit. A fun habit, but still.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.23: Behold! I rise... slowly... these bones aren't as young as they used to be. Give a lich a minute, will you?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.24: Ah, a fresh summoning. Please tell me you have foes. Or souls. Either works.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.25: Nice base you got here. Needs more skulls. Just saying.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.spawn.26: Welcome. I am known as the Nameless One. I have awaited your rise, just as was prophesied long ago. I have waited patiently for countless lifetimes, but it has all been worth it. You have finally arrived.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.1: The dead who have already passed cannot truly be killed.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.2: Death is my throne, not my end. You've merely given me a reason to recline.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.3: Kill one who is already dead? Like trying to drown a fish——the outcome was never in doubt.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.4: Death cannot kill death itself——just as a shadow cannot devour its own darkness.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.5: Congratulations, you killed me! Now give my servant a few minutes to reorganize my bones. Standard procedure.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.8: Death has threatened me once, it will not have a second chance.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.10: I see the victor standing in the light,But remember—the night is but a brief rest...
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.death.11: The power of undeath lies in my palm.You have merely released my eternal soul.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.1: I haven't eaten in centuries. Not because I'm dead, but because the local cuisine is terrible. Too much sand.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.2: You call that armor? My skeletons dress better than you, and they've been wearing the same outfit for 500 years.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.3: Life's a joke, and I've been dead long enough to appreciate the punchline.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.4: Life is like a skeleton's joke: no body gets it.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.5: Life is like a skeleton's bow: sometimes it miss, sometimes it shoots you in the back. Wait, that's oddly specific. Never mind.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.6: Dishonorable? Dude, I've been dead for millennia. Social conventions are for people with pulse.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.7: Read that scroll, are you? Smart move. I spent centuries learning to turn skeletons into necromancers the hard way. You get to skip straight to the good part. Life's not fair, and neither is undeath.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.8: Burial Scroll? Found one in my own pyramid once. Very embarrassing. Imagine someone reading your diary, except the diary turns skeletons into necromancers.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.9: Souls. Delicious, nutritious, and they never go out of style. It's like farming, but the crops scream. Adds flavor.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.10: Some people say 'live every day like it's your last.' I say 'live every day like it's someone else's last.' Much more entertaining that way.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.11: Beneath the sands where mortals tread, I rule the kingdom of the dead. My bones are old, my wit is keen, your armor's uglier than it seems.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.12: You know, for someone who keeps resurrecting me and making me stronger, you're not half bad. A third bad, maybe. A quarter on a good day. Don't let it go to your head.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.13: Sometimes I summon skeletons just to watch them try to figure out doors. It's been millennia and they still haven't gotten it. Best entertainment in the Nameless Kingdom.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.14: I could raise every corpse in a hundred blocks. Including that chicken you ate for dinner last week. Imagine that—a vengeful drumstick coming for you.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.15: Roses are red, violets are blue, I'm already dead, and soon you will be too. ...What? Too soon? It's never too soon in necromancy.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.16: Roses are red, violets are blue, I'll raise your grandma, and make her haunt you. ...What? You said poetry. I never promised good poetry.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.17: I could raise your entire family tree as zombies. And believe me, your great-aunt Edna (I made it up xd)still complains. About everything. You'll never have peace.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.18: Someone and I had an agreement. He'd get my army, my servants get protection from the sun. But you know what they say about deals with necromancers? We always collect. Eventually. Preferably with interest.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.19: Wealth? I have a pyramid full of gold. Do I use it? No. Do I let anyone else have it? Absolutely not. It's the principle of the thing.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.20: Nice threads. Wait, is that MY design? You're wearing MY look? ...I'm flattered. And slightly annoyed. But mostly flattered.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.21: Nice robe. Nice crown. Stealing my look? Flattering, but also copyright infringement. My lawyers are dead, but they're also very persistent.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.22: Hungry for souls? I know the feeling. It's like hunger, but existential. And it never goes away. Bon appétit!
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.23: The circle formation? Oh, that's my signature move. 'The Ring of 'Oh No''. Tactical advantage, psychological warfare, and it looks fabulous. No escape, no cover, just... death from all angles. I call it 'aggressive hugging'.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.24: Those Illager guys? Please. They wear pajamas to war and think they're intimidating. I've seen scarecrows with more presence. At least the crows respect those.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.25: Those Illagers? Amateurs. They pillage villages. I raise villages. Metaphorically. Sometimes literally, if they're built on an old cemetery.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.26: This staff? Oh, it's not just for show. It shoots, it summons, and it doubles as a backscratcher for those hard-to-reach vertebrae. Multitasking.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.27: Enchanting? Please, that's just librarians with too much time. True necromancy? Now that's an art form. It also glows better.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.28: You know what happens when you log off? No, don't worry. I'll wait. I've got nothing but time. Literally.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.29: Snow! Lovely. Nothing says 'I'm an undead overlord' like watching your skeleton minions try to build a snowman. Spoiler: they keep making snow-skeletons. It's adorable and mildly narcissistic.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.30: The Great War? The Piglins vs. Everyone? That was before my time. Missed all the drama. By the time I showed up, everyone was already mad at everyone else.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.31: Kneel before the Nameless One! ...Or don't. I'm not your mom. But it would be dramatically appropriate.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.32: 'Nameless One'? Creative, I know. The backstory is tragic: I had a name once, a beautiful, elegant name. But then I became so powerful, so terrifying, that merely speaking my name would cause villages to crumble. ...Or maybe I just forgot it. One of those two.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.33: You know, threatening you is like trying to scare a zombie with brains. Technically possible, but ultimately pointless.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.34: Humanity? Last I checked, I'm missing a few key components. Like skin. And a heartbeat. And the ability to enjoy pumpkin pie. ...Actually, I do miss pumpkin pie.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.35: You know, I could turn your bones into a xylophone. And then play a sad song on it. About you.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.36: Some days you raise an army of the dead, conquer a village, and feel like a true overlord. Other days, you wake up, find your crown is crooked, and just go back to sleep. Today is one of those days.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.37: Ah, the old kingdom. Great architecture, better necromancers, and the buffet was to die for. Literally.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.38: Glorious? We were so glorious that the sun itself asked us to tone it down a bit. We refused. Obviously.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.39: Reading a book about dark arts? Cute. It's like watching a chicken read a recipe book about itself.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.40: Reading a guide? How adorable. Back in my day, we learned necromancy by... well, by dying mostly. Trial and error. Literally trial and death.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.41: Enchanting? Cute. You wave some lapis lazuli around, mumble a few words, and hope for the best. I raise the dead and bend reality to my will. But sure, your Dark Anvil is very impressive. For a hobbyist.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.42: Soul energy? Oh, it's not as complicated as the living make it out to be. Think of it as... the world's weirdest currency. Better than emeralds, because try buying a zombie army with green rocks. Go ahead, I'll wait.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.43: Wardens have 10,000 soul energy. You know what that makes them? Walking ATMs. Very angry, very blind ATMs that will punch you into next Tuesday. But hey, high risk, high reward. That's necromancy in a nutshell.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.44: You know, every time you cast a spell with those souls, somewhere a villager sighs in relief. 'Thank goodness it was his soul and not mine... this time.'
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.45: Animals give five soul. It's a living. Or rather, it's a dying. For them, anyway.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.46: Kill the Wither? Five soul. The same as a chicken. I don't make the rules, I just laugh at them.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.47: A soul arca? Fancy word for 'soul piggy bank'. Though I have to admit, watching you hoard 100,000 souls like a dragon with golds is... endearing. In a terrifying way.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.48: Ah, the Transfer Gem. The marriage certificate between you and your soul storage. Beautiful. Just don't lose it. Soul divorce is messy.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.49: You cheat death so often I'm starting to think death is just... mildly annoyed at this point. 'Oh, it's YOU again. Fine, come back in. Tea's on the counter.'
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.50: You see, mastering one school of dark art is like eating only golden carrots. Boring. I prefer a buffet of dark arts. Necromancy for the main course, Magic as a side dish, and a pinch of Wind for dessert. Variety keeps you... undead.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.51: I have a focus collection. Bulwark focus for when I need to prevent my cloak from getting punctured, leeching focus for when I'm feeling peckish... Oh, and one that just makes a 'wololo' sound when you use it. Very important for fun.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.52: Wind blades. Fancy. They think adding 'air' makes it less evil? 'Oh no, I'm being sliced by gentle breezes!' No. Evil is evil. Pick a lane, guys.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.53: Wind blades. Sounds fancy, right? It's just sharpened air. Imagine being killed by a really aggressive breeze. 'How did you die?' 'Tuesday was a bit too windy.' Not the most dignified epitaph.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.54: The desert plague. It's rain, hail, and swarm all at once. Climate change finally weaponized.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.55: Trembling Focus，Repel and slow. Good for keeping adventurers at a distance. I prefer a more personal touch—like reminding them that their ancestors are watching from my army.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.56: You don't build an army with just one type of soldier. That's like making a sandwich with only bread. Boring and ineffective.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.57: Join my army! Benefits include: no taxes, no need to eat, and you get a free pair of cool glowing eyes. Downsides: you're dead. But hey, nobody's perfect.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.58: Vanguards? Oh, they're my pride and joy. Sharp as their blades, loyal as... well, as dead things can be. Just don't ask them to dance.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.59: Blackguards. The name says it all. They're slow, they're dumb, and they'll smash your problems into a fine paste. Think of them as the 'aggressive negotiations' option.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.60: Skeletons are the archery enthusiasts who never shut up about their bow technique. 'Oh, I hit a moving target from 50 blocks away!' Yes, Greg, we know. You've been dead for 300 years.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.61: Zombies are the interns of the undead world. No skills, no speed, but they show up every day. You have to respect the commitment.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.62: Wraiths. Ever wanted your enemies to literally feel the cold shoulder? Not fire, mind you. Fire is hot, passionate, so cliché. This is glacial flame—it burns cold, like your ex's heart.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.63: Reapers. See that scythe? It's not for farming wheat. Unless... your soul is wheat. Then yes, harvest time.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.64: I asked a reaper to help with gardening once. Now I have a very neat graveyard and absolutely no flowers. Lesson learned.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.65: You know what I love about husks? They don't complain about the heat. 'Oh, it's too sunny, I'm burning, I need a hat...' Shut up, you're dead. Meanwhile my husks are just vibing in the desert like 'Ah, feels like home.'
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.66: You know those magicians who never shut up about the 'good old days'? Imagine that, but they're mummified and every story starts with 'back when I had skin...'
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.67: Parched shoots weakness arrows. How considerate! Instead of killing you, they just make you feel as pathetic as they look.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.68: Ancient Gong, huh? You bang it and they show up. Imagine being summoned like that. 'Quick, someone's banging a gong!' 'Ugh, again? Tell them I'm dead. ...Wait, I am dead. Fine, let's go.'
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.69: Ancient gong, huh? Of course. You don't just text a 2000-year-old mage. You have to make an entrance. Ding-dong, your doom is here—with impeccable posture.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.70: Parched Necromancers were scholars once. Read books, wrote scrolls, debated philosophy. Now they debate whether to hit you with wind or a tornado. Some things never change—they still love the sound of their own voice. Metaphorically. They don't have vocal cords anymore.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.71: 'Creatures of ancient ghosts unwilling to sink into darkness.' Sounds like someone had too much time on their hands. Or afterlife. Whatever.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.72: I don't think you need my power for the battle itself—if you truly want strength, those detachable war machines would suit you better. You just want to watch a... grand performance?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.73: Life's short. Then it gets long. Then it gets eternal and you're stuck with me. Really makes you rethink those life choices, huh?
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.74: Life's a joke, death's a punchline, and I'm the one laughing from the audience. Badum-tss!
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.75: I heard there's marble who's 'very tasty and juicy'. Is that a new player? Can I own one? For research purposes.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.76: That Marb1e dude... always so watery. If he shows up, I feel like my pyramid needs a better drainage system. Stop flowing, dude!
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.77: Player_7? Punch bosses like they owe her money? I have nightmares about her. And I don't even sleep! That's how terrifying he is.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.78: They say Player_7 fights bosses without a single hit. I say that's creepy. Very, very creepy.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.79: I should visit k1sak142 sometime. You know, just to... thank him personally. With my army. And my best green smoke. It'll be a party.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.80: k1sak142? Oh, he's the reason I exist. Also the reason I have to deal with all of you. Respect where it's due, I suppose.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.81: To be or not to be has never been a question worth pondering,For they are ultimately one and the same.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.82: You and I may be of the same kind, so come,let us seek the true meaning of eternity.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.83: Weapons or magic... What you have seen, what you have not,I have set foot upon them all.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.84: What is the purpose of life, you ask? Meaningless pursuits,Meaningless joys and sorrows, meaningless heartbeats and breaths...Only change never changes,just as only death possesses infinite life.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.85: My heart is empty, yet possesses all,for I have grasped the eternal mystery,and no longer need the shackles of the mortal world.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.86: Food, beauty, honor—true sages cast them away.No hunger, no lust, no desire for power—Only a great will flows forth!
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.87: This is my idle animation. Very intimidating, right? Just floating here, thinking about... what's for dinner. souls, probably. Always souls.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.88: I've been watching you farm. Fascinating. Imagine my undead army with farming skills. 'Zombie, till row three. Skeleton, water crops.' Genius.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.89: Age isn't everything. An old bone buried for eons isn't necessarily tougher than a hungry touhou little fairy maid.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.90: Magic domain GOAT? That's me, the Nameless One. Sorry Wine Fox, you've been outclassed.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.91: Poor Vizier. All that power, a ruby that amplifies spells, and what does he eat? Whatever illagers consider 'cuisine'. I'd rather chew on soul sand.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.92: Have you seen what passes for 'fine dining' in a woodland mansion? Creamy berry fish paste dumpling with chocolate sauce! No wonder he's always sad.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.93: I could turn you into a skeleton, but honestly? You'd probably complain about the lack of health insurance.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.94: Just a little secret, Dark Mage. The Vindicator chefs in the Woodland Mansion marinate ravager meat with swamp crocodile entrails, then add loads of snap warts vinegar. It's a delicacy among the Illagers.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.95: Herobrine? Removed? Please. You don't 'remove' a ghost story. You just stop telling it to the tourists.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.96: You know, the Mojang patch notes always say 'Removed Herobrine'. I do the same with my skeletons when they annoy me. 'Removed Skeleton #47'. Very therapeutic.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.97: Ur-Tsar? Now THAT'S a bloke who knows his way around a kitchen. His Nether stew? Fiery. Literally. Still warms the bones, even when you don't have any flesh left.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.98: I asked Ur-Tsar why he cooks. He said even apostles get tired of eating raw Nether Wart. Fair point. Even a lich needs a good meal now and then.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.99: Ur-Tsar, best chef in the Nether.His lava chicken is to die for-literally.Last batch raised three skeletons.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.chat.100: A roast laowang237 a day keeps the baka away.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.player_dea: Oh, did I do that? No, wait, I wasn't even there. Guess you're just... naturally talented at failing.
Don't worry, death happens to everyone. Some more often than others. Like you. Definitely you.
You're really committed to this whole 'researching death' thing, aren't you? Ten out of ten for dedication.
Don't worry, I'll make sure your bones are properly organized. Skeleton style: rib cage here, skull there, random toes everywhere.
You know, most people try to avoid dying. You seem to be collecting it like it's a hobby.
Ooh, a new ghost! Wait, no, you respawned. How disappointingly... alive.
Finally! I was getting bored watching you 'play it safe'. Much more entertaining now.
Back from the dead already? I'm flattered you're trying to imitate my lifestyle, but you're doing it wrong. You're supposed to stay dead and look fabulous doing it.
Death? Again? You're making my minions look overachievers. They only die twice. Usually.
Interesting strategy, dying right there. Really adds character to the landscape. Though I preferred the spot you died in yesterday, better lighting.
Respawn's a heck of a drug, isn't it? Don't worry, I won't tell anyone how you died.
Remember, kids: in Minecraft, you can respawn. But your pride? That save file is corrupted.
Respawning? How many times must I teach you this lesson, old man?
Respawning? Sure, take your time. I'll be here, judging your life choices.
Tell death I said hi. We're on a first-name basis.
Your respawn timer is shorter than my attention span. See you in 5... 4...
It's a beautiful day outside. Birds are singing, flowers are blooming. On days these, it's a shame you dark mage's not burning in hell.
Wasted.
Task failed successfully? No, wait, that's not how it works. Better luck next respawn!
Well done. Here come the test results: 'You are a horrible person.' That's what it says: a horrible person. We weren't even testing for that.
'You Died.' Fancy font. Very dramatic. Almost makes up for the lack of skill.
Ooooh, that looked like it hurt! ...Wait, it did? Oh. My bad. ...Not sorry.
Skill issue.
Game over, man! GAME OVER!
I used to be an adventurer like you. Then I took a... well, you know the rest.
That's one small step for a man, one giant pile of dead for mankind.
Hey, on the bright side: no hospital bills in Minecraft. Just walk it off.
Please stay determined! Or not lol.
Respawn, retry, rage quit. The holy trinity of your gameplay.
From hero to zero. Told you that quote would be useful someday.
GG. EZ. — Oh sorry, reflex. Even undead can't resist the taunt, occupational hazard.
Dark souls... please take this... oh it's you. Regular customer, just find a spot to lie down.
Don't worry, I know a great resurrection spell. ...Oh wait, that's YOUR job.
One moment you're commanding legions, the next you're commanding... dirt. The circle of life. Or undeath. Whatever.
So, where's your new spawn point? Bed didn't explode, I hope? Good. I'll have my skeletons burn some paper offerings... wait, no paper here. How about some planks?
Well, well, well. If it isn't the consequence of your own actions. I'd say 'I told you so', but my non-existent lips are sealed.
In my millennia of career, I've learned one truth: the living always think they can win. They're wrong.
Need me to summon some skeletons for a funeral procession? I can make them dance too. Gotta set the mood right.
Death is not the end, only being forgotten is. But honestly, I'll probably forget you in about five minutes. So... you know.
Is this worker's comp? ...Oh right, I'm a villain, we don't do that.
Achievement unlocked: 'Philanthropist' – Donated your soul to the local necromancer.
Your death message has been added to Polarice3's changelog: 'Fixed a bug where the player was breathing'.
'You have died. Continue?'... Here, the answer is: 'Sure, as a skeleton.'
Minecraft death messages are usually boring. 'Player fell from a high place'? Too generic. Should be 'Player tried to prove they're better at not dying than the Nameless One, and failed.'
This is why I don't play on Hardcore mode.
Care to leave a 5-star review on the death screen?
Achievement unlocked: Die Like a Nobody. Oh wait, you already are one.
Server lag? No, you lag.
At least your death screen is better looking than the Someone's robe. That's something.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.summon_ser: I was in the middle of plotting evil things. This better be good.
You're telling me you walked all the way here, through zombies and spiders, just to ask me for that? Priorities, mate.
You want skeletons? I can do skeletons. You want zombies? Easy. You want them to do your taxes? That's where I draw the line.
You want an army? Fine. But if one of them complains about back pain or asks for a pension, they're your problem now.
You want an army? Say please. No, say it like you mean it. No, still not convincing. ...Fine, I'll do it anyway. I'm a softie.
Name them if you must, but don't get attached. They're like goldfish—replaceable, and with similarly short memory spans.
Rise and shine, my little bundles of existential dread! Your boss—that's me—has some work for you. No, not world domination. Again. Something even more tedious: helping the new boss.
Greedy, aren't we? But I like that. Just don't blame me if they have better manners than you.
Come! Come! Follow me, you who rise from the waterlogged earth! At last you have found your place, my undying servants!
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.discover_e: Intruders? Excellent. My vanguards were getting bored playing chess with their own skulls.
Well well well, if it isn't the main course wandering in before the appetizer.
Let me guess, they're here to 'save the day'? Spoiler alert: the day doesn't want to be saved. No one.
Let me guess—they're here to 'defeat the evil dark magician'? How... original.
Duty calls. And by duty, I mean violence. And by calls, I mean my blackguards are already on their way.
Friends of yours? No? Good. I was hoping for some fresh... recruits.
Death is just the beginning. Actually, in this case, it's the middle. You missed the beginning.
Stand back dude. Let me show you why they put me at the end of a dungeon as a final boss.
Ah, fresh meat! And by fresh I mean... well, not for long.
Ooh, visitors! Should I put the kettle on? Or should I put the 'raise the dead' on?
Ah, fresh meat delivery! And here I thought they didn't serve my order.
Oh, I sense something with a pulse nearby. How... irritating.
You hear that? That's the sound of someone about to have a very bad day. And I'm not talking about myself.
Ah, fresh meat! My undead have been eating nothing but dust for centuries. They deserve takeout.
Ah, fresh... uh... whatever they are. I hope they drop something good.
Ooh, a visitor! Should I prepare the red carpet or the grave dirt?
I sense something... annoying. Which is basically all living things.
Remember lads, no refunds, no returns, and definitely no surviving.
Fee-fi-fo-fum, I smell the blood of a living one. Wait, that's not my line. Whatever, still applicable.
So... let the show begin.
Here, taste this.
Gotcha.
Return to dust... (whispered)
Darkness reigns～the north wind sings～now DANCE! (chanting)
A relic of the old age, versus the refuse of the new? Intriguing.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_enemy: They came, they saw, they got conquered. Classic.
Another one bites the dust! Get it? Because skeletons don't have... oh never mind, my humor is too advanced for the living.
Look at them crumble. Literally. I love it when they do that.
They put up a fight. A very short, very pointless fight.
Rest in pieces! Get it? Because they... oh, you got it the first time. Smart audience.
Another victory for the undead! We're really racking up the bodies. Literally racking them. In piles.
Even in death, there's always a bigger fish. Or in this case, a bigger pile of bones.
Ah, the sweet sound of bones rattling in victory. Music to my... non-existent ears.
Are you grinding? Probably. Or you're just lazy and can't fight him on your own. Oh well.
Tell me, was that really the best they could send? I've seen more intimidating frogs.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_speci: Ah, the old crone. She brewed potions for decades, and in the end, she couldn't brew an escape plan. My servants send their regards—and their thanks for the hat.
A crone? Please. She was just an old granny with a potion addiction. It's better to sent her to the ultimate retirement home.
He rallied the illagers, blocked with his stick, dreamed of power. Now he's just... unelected. Permanently.
Another 'great leader' bites the dust. At least his dramatic death pose was entertaining. 7/10 for style.
It climbed, it summoned maggots, it made creepy noises. In the end, it just couldn't 'wight' enough to survive.
This one was hungry for souls. Relatable, honestly. But rude to steal from my mate's pantry. So annoying...
Oh, the 'Lord' of a single cramped tomb? Cute title. Back in my day, we didn't call someone 'Lord' unless they could curse an entire kingdom. Now look at him. Dust in a tin can.
All that fuss over a pot. He was more attached to that jar than a crimson mosquito is to flesh. Good riddance.
Commanding wither skeletons, summoning flames... It had potential. But it got hung up on the past. The 'good old days' in the fortress. Me? I look forward. To working for you, my mate, apparently.
Burnt to a crisp by his own research. The irony is so thick you could spread it on bread. My mate, on the other hand, knows how to handle power without getting singed.
Clank, clank, boom. That's all it does. Walk, punch, drop some glowing pebbles. My skeletons have better aim and better conversation. At least they can groan on key. Pathetic.
A Redstone Golem? Pfft. Just a big, angry rock with placing mines issues. The illagers stole the blueprint, but they forgot to steal a personality to go with it.
I killed... that? A Brood Mother? You're sure it wasn't just an overgrown silverfish with anger issues?
Please tell me you didn't raise me from my eternal nap just to witness the demise of that... mini boss embarrassment.
Uh, what's this? A slightly bigger bug than a bug? Still just a bug, I see. Ahem.
Ah, an Endersent! Now that has some heft. Almost made me put down my ectoplasm dessert. Almost.
Finally, something that hits back. ...Well, tried to hit back. Points for effort, I suppose.
He was always so dramatic with that 'ice-fire' of his. Told him it would leave him cold in the end. Get it? Cold? ...No? Just me?
That wraith thought he was so clever, hiding in his icy little hut. I just reminded him who he works for.
He used to complain about the heat in the pyramid. I told him, 'At least you're not a snow golem.' Now he's not complaining at all. Problem solved.
At least he went out with a breeze. Several of them, actually.
Hot-headed little fellow, wasn't he? All that fire and fury... and poof. Like a candle in the wind. A very angry, flammable candle.
Four shields and all that bluster, and he still couldn't protect himself from a friendly reminder of his place.

That ruby amplified his power, they say. Amplified his mediocrity, more like. All that arcane potential, wasted on a creature whose only talent is looking down on others from a slightly higher platform.
The 'Vizier'? Look at that gaudy cloak, look at that panicked expression. High rank, sure, but his power doesn't match his ambition, and even the cafeteria food is too good for his 'noble' status.

Ooooh, look at me, I'm the Vizier! I can summon fangs and fly through walls! ...And I'm also incredibly under-qualified for my job. That grand explosion at the end? Just for show. Like everything else about him.
Did you hear that crunch? That's the sound of a 'monstrosity' being downgraded to a 'minor inconvenience'. Who's next on the list?
You know, for a walking mountain of rocks and rage, it had a very simple weakness: me. It just didn't know it until it was too late.
You know, between you and me, I'm almost glad that thing was locked away. One of nature's... shall we say, 'enthusiastic' experiments. My compliments to the ancient chefs who couldn't digest it.
This power... I must admit, it's more ancient and savage than I imagined. Fighting it was like wrestling with the untamed forces of all three dimensions. A foe worth remembering.
Oh, switching to the Coral Spear now? Or was it the Dagger of Venom? Frankly, by the time she made up her mind, my legion had already made up hers. Next time, bring a bigger anchor.
Drowned in the deep for eternity, yet still she fought. Now, she's truly part of the scenery.
All that fire, all that rage... and it still couldn't handle a bit of superior dark arts. Size isn't everything, as they say.
Next time, send me after something challenging. A slightly angry black beast, perhaps. At least it would put up a fight worth mentioning.

This thing tried to confuse me by splitting its body? Please, using decoys is my signature trick. I could tell which part was the head at a glance, and then… snap. The rest wiggled just like a frightened earthworm.
All that cosmic light, and you still couldn't see death coming from behind my shots. Next time, try opening your eyes. Oh wait, there is no next time.
Oh, the great Frostmaw, terror of the tundra? Please. Any necromancer's homework could take it down easily.
This furry popsicle thought its frost could trouble the Nameless One? My bones have chilled in graves colder than this for millennia.
It flapped, it glowed, it threw a tantrum... and then it went poof. Very dramatic for something that's now just expensive mask.
This 'leader of the Umuthana' spent its final moments confused as to why its light didn't bother us. Spoiler: we're the ones who put out the lights.
Seriously, buddy, you just wanted to see what I'd say, right?
Well, that was a refreshing nap interrupted. Next time, wake me for something that actually tries.
Turning red and angry? Cute strategy. My go-to move is just pointing my staff and... well, you saw what happened.
Another lich? Please. They had potential—emphasis on 'had.' Maybe you can dig through those ash piles and find something useful.
Another one bites the dust. Literally. For beings that cheat death, they die an awful lot. Maybe invest in a better insurance plan? Just a thought.
A boss? Please. The cavities in my grandmother's teeth had more boss potential than this shroom-headed cow.
I know, this is just one in a billion of your power. Be afraid, folks. I'm already seriously scared to tears.
I've studied necromantic aesthetics for eons, but its crooked teeth were an abomination. Now that it's quiet, the world feels balanced again.
I'm really getting bored, mate. Seriously.
All that ice, all those crystals... and she still melted under pressure. Literally.
She kept screaming 'You'll never reach the top floor!' and I thought... lady, I don't need stairs. I'm dead. I float.
I think I used up all my luck for this millennium. And the next two. So if you ever want me to fight anything like this, the answer is no. Now, seriously, tesseract, run.
Lucky? No, no, no. Luck implies chance. This was... a statistically improbable alignment of celestial bodies that favored my general direction.
Dude, this is getting awkward. Am I the murderer or the victim here? My lawyer is going to have a field day.
Great, now we've proven the Nameless One can be defeated. Wait, doesn't that make me vulnerable?
You're pitting 'me' against 'me'? How amusing. Which 'me' do you think will walk away?
Your lot really is getting weaker and weaker... Must I be told exactly where to strike before I hit you? Are you a cult or a circus troupe?
Once a king, a lord, or perhaps... the village idiot? Doom is the only promotion you deserve, and I've just handed it to you.
That false prophet was throwing around 'sacred fire' like cheap confetti. My boneheads showed him the difference between a spark and the cold, eternal ember.
It seems I can't tell front from back after twisting my head off. Guess that makes it harder for you to sneak up on me, eh? (Bony chuckle)
No bones to pick, no flesh to rot. Just... dignity and duty. A clean death for a clean fighter.
His sword didn't waver once. Even at the end. I almost feel... honored. Almost. Now, his vigil is over, and he can finally rest, without the void whispering in his ear.
Back in my day, 'Dead Kings' had to earn their title. Nowadays, they just hand them out with a spell book and a bad attitude.
You see that? That's how a real professional handles the competition. No hard feelings, though. He can have the crypt. I've got a kingdom.
Enraged? Oh no, please, I'm shaking in my non-existent boots. Nothing says 'intimidating' like a king who can't control his temper.
You can't spell 'Enraged Dead King' without 'Dead'... and guess what? He's the only part of that name that's still accurate.
Disinfecting by fire? For most undead, flames are indeed terrifying. But you know—death doesn't burn.
First Flamebearer? Cute title. Let me show you what happens to the 'First' when you've got eternity to wait for the 'Last'.
He offered his soul to the Void for the one he loved. Were I a mere mortal, I'd praise his sacrifice. But alas, I am not.
He gave his all for the one he loved. He fought, he adapted, he became his own prison. But in the end, the Void doesn't send thank-you notes. It just takes. I, at least, offer a swift exit.
A little trinket from a Nether refugee? Step aside. I've had my laugh.
Tell you a joke, I once had a pet rock. It had more personality. This thing is just its cousin who peaked in high school and never moved out of the forge.
Don't run, little one. That little confident of your own abilities, are you?
Is that a Shulker? ...Under all that? Apparently. Talk about an identity crisis. At least I know I'm a bony ruler of a kingdom, not a clam in a robot suit.
Your decorating taste and your combat skills have one thing in common: they're both a few grains of sand away from total extinction. Trust me, nobody knows the desert better than me.
Death Finds a Way. But I am Death itself.
Remember when I said I command legions of the undead? This. This is why. Because we do the heavy lifting while you watch. You're welcome.
You know, for a 'harbinger' of something, it didn't really... herald much. Just a lot of noise and laser pointers.
The dead monarch shouldn't interfere with the affairs of the living... Oh wait, that applies to me too...
Oh, head explosion. Nice effect, gotta book that for the Nameless Kingdom annual gala. Beats my skeleton vanguards constantly dropping their jaws.
So-called draconic might. Nothing more. Even a true dragon wouldn't dare defy death—let alone its handmaiden.
My lady. The storm is over. ...Oh wait, she's gone. Pity. She had a certain... electrifying charm. Reminds me of my ex. Also high maintenance, also full of static. Just kidding, dude.
It could rend the very barriers of space. Yet its barren mind could grasp nothing beyond mindless consumption.
'The abyss is staring at you'? Please. I'm the one looking down on the abyss now. And honestly, its stare hurts less than my rearguard's glare.
The lingering soul of the ancient battlefield rekindled you from the ashes. So I've come to snuff out that guttering flame.
His flames once lit the arena. A warrior who rises from ash deserves to be remembered, not just defeated.
I admire that spider. It knows what it wants: revenge, then a meal. Way better than some freak who just throws candies. Now watch a real master handle the aftermath.
Ah, a 'brilliant' alchemist who created something he couldn't control. And got eaten for his trouble. How... poetic. Now, where's my giant arachnid opponent?
So the spider's dead. Its final trick? A face of villager soul. Adorable. But not as adorable as the idea of resurrecting its master to work for you. Imagine his face when he saw you again.
Remember, kids: In the Nameless Kingdom, we don't fear bugs that climb walls with villager faces. We make bugs fear walls full of villager skulls.
Magispeller? Sounds like a spelling bee contestant. Clearly, he never learned to spell 'D-E-F-E-A-T'. My skeletons, however, are great at spelling 'L-O-O-T'.

I've seen many mages. Ones who called themselves lords while trapped in pithos. Ones who cast their barriers under eternal twilight. Even that fool with the Orb of Dominance. This dude? A mere 'magic enthusiast'.
All flash, no substance. Wasting dozens of souls just to put on a fireworks show? If any of my minions pulled that stunt, they'd be back in their jars faster than you can say 'smoothie'.
Another spiritual expert who forgot that spirits need bodies to do anything useful. Mine come with bones. Much more practical.
A talentless floater with a gimmick of knocking things away—quite the match. He's finally free from gravity now... assuming you'd even bother to bound this fool.
Another Invoker? Please. The only thing they invoke is a mild inconvenience.
Four brains stitched together, and not an ounce of wit. But that muscle? Well-marbled, I'll give it that.You know, its keeper had more talent than those grey-skins—at least in the culinary arts.
This so-called Wilden Chimera fought like an angry bunny. But same result—ends up in the ground.
These fools journey to the End just to come back with... purple highlights? Very edgy.
Watch them fade. Purple's not their color anyway.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_playe: I suddenly feel so tired. Good luck, goodbye.
No need to be afraid. Perhaps he had another choice—too bad the rearguards were already full, and he probably wasn't cut out for studying spells anyway. His better future lies in a shade gravestone.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_ender: You call that an end? Not even its own species could end it.
The End? Maybe take another look around. You might find a little surprise—say, atop that shoddy altar.
So it ends. Shame about the bones, though. They were decent.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_withe: Oh, the Wither? Three heads and not a single brain cell between them. I've taught necromancers with more charm.
I've seen explosions, I've seen chaos, but that thing was just showing off. Death should be elegant, not a firework display.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_warde: That blind beast finally shut up. It'd think being able to crush most things would come with some dignity, but no—it just screams in the dark like a toddler who lost his favorite toy.
Sculk sensors detected movement, detected vibrations, detected... oh wait, they detected your defeat. How embarrassing.
goetyawaken.namelessone.quote.kill_guard: A sponge? How fitting. Let's soak up all those tears you must be crying right now.
Several spikes, one eye, and zero wins. For a monument guardian, you're more of a monument to failure.
